architecture_fit.note: Computes the exact A2@A1 product so it solves the literal task perfectly, but it is the trivial closed-form answer with no base_model.py delta, transformer, or learned/circuit mechanism.

baseline_comparison.note: Framework single-hop baseline (A2 alone) is surfaced clearly failing (0.22 at alpha 0.1) while the product holds ~1.0; lift-over-baseline shown across the full sweep.

faithfulness.note: No model, no ablation, no activation patching; purely the analytic computation, and the README does not explicitly flag this or propose a causal check.

operating_range.note: Sweep spans alpha 0.1–10.0 (>2 orders of peakedness) and fidelity stays ~1.0 throughout, though trivially since the product is exact.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Mechanism is fully hand-written (non-learned) and reproduces the target exactly, but it is the literal formula rather than a hand-set transformer circuit demonstrating understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab is an empty static-markdown placeholder (no gr.Tab, no heatmaps of A1/A2/A_chain); only the shared benchmark_panel carries any signal.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the benchmark-panel axes and the baseline contrast sensibly, but leans entirely on the shared dashboard and claims 'two tabs' the app does not actually build.

notes: The attempt hits a perfect benchmark by having model_fn compute exactly A2@A1 — the same product task.py uses as ground truth — so fidelity and composition_robustness are ~1.0 everywhere. It is mechanically correct and beats the single-hop baseline cleanly, but it is the trivial closed-form answer: no transformer/base_model delta, no faithfulness/ablation evidence, and a placeholder Demo tab that doesn't visualize the composition. Mean of scored items ≈3.0 → borderline.
